scenario_summary: One host runs an installed stable channel and a portable_stable channel from a self-contained directory at the same time. The installed channel registers its update marker, recent items, file associations, and protocol-handler scheme as usual; the portable channel keeps every state root and marker under its portable directory and registers no file association, no protocol handler, and no machine-global recent-item entry on the host. Neither channel writes the other's state.

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: The installed Aureline and the portable Aureline run side by side. The portable copy keeps everything inside its folder; it does not change file associations, deep-link handlers, or your operating system's recent-files list. Removing the portable folder removes the portable install completely.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: Installed-and-portable coexistence is supported. Portable mode never registers file associations, protocol handlers, or machine-global recent-item entries; the portable directory is the only state surface portable mode owns.
explanation_fields.support_summary: Support exports for the portable install resolve through state.portable_colocated_root.portable_stable; support exports for the installed channel resolve through the per-user roots. The two never share a support bundle.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Promote-eligible. The pair.installed_and_portable admission row enforces zero machine-global mutation by portable mode; the install-topology matrix's portable_no_machine_global_mutation rule pins the same constraint at the install layer.

notes: Reference fixture for portable side-by-side installs.
